source_item.title: Zepto IPO anchor book nears closure; Norges, Motilal Oswal may take 40-45%

slide1_hook.label: Two Stanford Dropouts Are About to List
slide1_hook.subhead: Zepto's ₹11,000 crore IPO anchor book is nearly locked in.

slide2_backstory.label: THE BACKSTORY
slide2_backstory.headline: From Lockdown Grocery Problem to Unicorn
slide2_backstory.body: Zepto started in 2020 as KiranaKart, a grocery app from teen founders Aadit Palicha and Kaivalya Vohra stuck in Mumbai lockdowns. They dropped out of Stanford, renamed it Zepto, and built a 10-minute delivery network that made it a unicorn by 2023.

slide3_news.label: THE UPDATE
slide3_news.headline: Anchor Book Nearing Close at $5.1 Billion
slide3_news.body: Zepto's anchor book for its ₹11,000 crore IPO is almost final, with Norges and Motilal Oswal set to take 40-45% of it. The IPO values Zepto at $5.1 billion, down from $7 billion just months ago.

slide4_why.label: WHY IT MATTERS
slide4_why.headline: First Pure-Play Quick Commerce IPO in India
bullets: First quick-commerce company to list in India
Valuation cut shows investors want profits, not just growth
Could set the valuation bar for Blinkit and Instamart

slide5_engage.label: YOUR TAKE
slide5_engage.headline: Zepto is going public at $5.1 billion, nearly $2 billion below its valuation from months ago. Is that a healthy reality check, or a warning sign for quick commerce?
slide5_engage.cta: Follow for daily Indian business stories that actually matter. Drop your take in the comments 👇
